title: Positive vs. Negative Prompts
concept: Positive prompts describe what you want to see; negative prompts describe what to avoid, both steering the classifier-free guidance process.

exercises.prompt: When you ask an AI art generator for a picture, can you also tell it what NOT to include?

options.text: Yes, that's what negative prompts are for
No, AI only listens to what you want
Only humans can avoid things, not AI

exercises.explain: That's right! You can tell an AI what to include AND what to leave out. That's the idea behind negative prompts!

cards.title: What if you could tell AI what to skip?
cards.body: Imagine ordering a pizza. You say what toppings you want (pepperoni!) AND what to leave off (no onions!). AI image generators work the same way — you can describe what you want AND what you don't want, all in one request!

cards.title: Positive vs. Negative Prompts
cards.body: A positive prompt tells the AI what you DO want in the image, like 'a happy dog in a park.' A negative prompt tells the AI what to AVOID, like 'no blurry, no extra legs.' Together, they guide the AI's image-making process toward a cleaner, better result.

cards.title: Like Giving Directions
cards.body: Positive prompts are like saying 'turn left at the bakery' — clear directions toward your goal. Negative prompts are like saying 'don't go down the road with construction' — steering away from problems. Both help you reach the right destination faster!

cards.title: See It In Action
cards.body: Positive prompt: 'a cute cat sitting on a windowsill, sunny day, detailed fur.' Negative prompt: 'blurry, extra paws, dark, cartoon.' The AI uses both to draw a clear, realistic, well-lit cat with the correct number of paws!

cards.title: Myth: Negative Prompts Are Useless
cards.body: Some people think only positive prompts matter. Not true! Negative prompts help AI avoid common mistakes like extra fingers, weird faces, or blurry backgrounds. Skipping them often means messier, less accurate images.

cards.title: Two Prompts, One Picture
cards.body: Positive and negative prompts work together like a steering wheel — one pulls the image toward your idea, the other pushes it away from mistakes.

left.text: 'Bright, colorful, happy puppy, high detail' — tells AI what to create

right.text: 'Blurry, dark, extra legs, low quality' — tells AI what to avoid

exercises.prompt: What does a negative prompt do?

options.text: Tells the AI what to avoid in the image
Makes the AI generate a sad picture

exercises.explain: Correct! A negative prompt lists things you don't want to see, like 'blurry' or 'extra fingers,' helping guide the AI away from mistakes.

exercises.prompt: True or False: You can only use a positive prompt OR a negative prompt, never both together.
exercises.explain: False! Most AI image tools let you use both at the same time — positive prompts describe what you want, negative prompts describe what to avoid, working together.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the idea:
exercises.sentence: A ___ prompt tells the AI what you want to see, like 'a colorful sunset.'
exercises.explain: A positive prompt describes what you want in the image, guiding the AI toward that goal.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is an example of a negative prompt?

options.text: 'no blurry, no extra fingers, no watermark'
'a golden retriever playing fetch in a park'

exercises.explain: Right! That option lists things to avoid — the classic style of a negative prompt.

exercises.prompt: You're using an AI art app. You want a photo of a superhero, but you keep getting blurry images with extra arms. What should you add to fix this?

options.text: A negative prompt like 'blurry, extra arms, low quality'
Nothing, AI mistakes can't be fixed
A longer positive prompt with more colors only
Turn off the internet and try again

exercises.explain: Exactly! Adding a negative prompt like 'blurry, extra arms, low quality' tells the AI exactly what mistakes to avoid, helping it create a cleaner, more accurate superhero image.
